description:
Dogfood feedback from a real `/bw delegate` run showed two operator-visibility gaps even after truthful merge-back landed: (1) `worker.log` became nearly useless after switching workers to non-interactive `pi --print`, often containing only the final message, leaving long stretches where the operator cannot tell whether the worker is progressing or stuck; (2) the parent session may emit the 'ticket closed, waiting for process exit' notice but then stay silent while validation/landing runs, so the operator does not know when post-exit orchestration starts or when it fully completes.

Scope:
- make delegated worker launch/log output useful enough for humans during long silent periods
- add explicit parent-session guidance immediately after `/bw delegate` so the operator knows what happens next
- emit follow-up notifications for the major end stages (worker exited, validation/landing started, final landed/attention result)
- add regression coverage for the notification/logging flow
title: Make delegated worker progress and completion notifications operator-visible
